description: Text must have sufficient contrast against its background — 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold)
check_method: Use browser DevTools color picker or axe-core contrast checker on all text elements
common_violation: Light gray text (#999) on white background gives only 2.8:1 ratio
fix: Change text color to #595959 or darker to achieve 4.5:1 minimum ratio

description: Enhanced contrast requires 7:1 for normal text and 4.5:1 for large text
check_method: Same as 1.4.3 but with stricter thresholds — check all body text especially
common_violation: Body text at #666 on white gives 5.7:1 which passes AA but fails AAA
fix: Use #404040 or darker for body text to achieve 7:1 ratio for AAA

description: UI components and graphical objects must have 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent colors
check_method: Check borders of input fields and icons and chart elements against their backgrounds
common_violation: Input field with #ccc border on white background gives only 1.6:1 ratio
fix: Change input border to #767676 or darker to achieve 3:1 minimum

description: All functionality must be operable through keyboard without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes
check_method: Tab through entire page — every interactive element must be reachable and activatable via keyboard
common_violation: Custom dropdown built with div and span elements has no keyboard support — cannot Tab to it or use Arrow keys
fix: Use native select or add tabindex and role=listbox with keydown handlers for Arrow and Enter and Escape keys

description: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component the focus can also be moved away using only keyboard
check_method: Tab into every component and verify you can Tab out — especially modals and custom widgets
common_violation: Modal dialog traps focus and Tab cycles only within modal but Escape does not close it
fix: Add Escape key handler to close modal and return focus to the trigger element

description: Any keyboard-operable UI has a visible focus indicator
check_method: Tab through page and verify every focused element shows a visible outline or ring
common_violation: CSS includes outline: none or outline: 0 globally removing all focus indicators
fix: Add focus-visible styles with 2px solid outline offset by 2px in a contrasting color

description: When a UI component receives focus the focus indicator is not entirely hidden by author-created content
check_method: Check that sticky headers and footers and floating elements do not cover focused items
common_violation: Sticky navigation bar covers the focused element when Tabbing through items near the top of the viewport
fix: Add scroll-padding-top equal to sticky header height or use scroll-margin on focusable elements

description: Headings must follow a logical hierarchical order without skipping levels
check_method: Inspect DOM for heading elements and verify h1 then h2 then h3 order with no skips
common_violation: Page has h1 then jumps to h4 then back to h2 — heading hierarchy is broken
fix: Restructure headings to follow sequential order: h1 for page title then h2 for sections then h3 for subsections

description: All non-decorative images must have alternative text that describes the image content or purpose
check_method: Check every img element for alt attribute — informative images need descriptive alt and decorative images need alt="" or aria-hidden=true
common_violation: Product image shows img src=product.jpg with no alt attribute — screen reader announces file name
fix: Add descriptive alt text: alt="Blue running shoe Nike Air Max side view" or alt="" if purely decorative

description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its context
check_method: Check all links for descriptive text — avoid generic text that gives no context
common_violation: Multiple links on page all say "Click here" or "Read more" with no distinguishing context
fix: Use descriptive link text: "Read the accessibility guidelines" or add aria-label="Read more about accessibility"

description: Every form input must have a programmatically associated label element
check_method: Check that every input has a label with matching for and id attributes or is wrapped in a label element
common_violation: Input field uses placeholder="Email" as the only label — disappears when user types and is not announced by screen readers
fix: Add visible label element: label for=email followed by input id=email with placeholder as supplementary hint only

description: Custom interactive elements must have appropriate ARIA attributes to convey name and role and state
check_method: Check custom components for proper role and aria-label and aria-expanded and aria-selected attributes
common_violation: Custom toggle switch is a styled div with onClick but has no role=switch or aria-checked attribute
fix: Add role=switch and aria-checked=true/false and aria-label="Enable notifications" to the toggle element

description: Interactive targets must be at least 24x24 CSS pixels with 48x48 recommended
check_method: Measure clickable element dimensions including padding — check on mobile viewport
common_violation: Small icon button is 16x16px with no padding making it nearly impossible to tap accurately on mobile
fix: Add padding to make the hit area at least 48x48px or use min-width and min-height: 48px on the button

description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple pages
check_method: Check for skip link as first focusable element — it should be hidden until focused
common_violation: No skip link present — keyboard users must Tab through entire navigation on every page load
fix: Add a skip link as first element in body: a href=#main class=sr-only focus:not-sr-only followed by main id=main

description: The default human language of each page can be programmatically determined
check_method: Check html element for lang attribute with valid language code
common_violation: HTML tag has no lang attribute — screen readers cannot determine correct pronunciation language
fix: Add lang="th" or lang="en" to the html element matching the primary content language

description: If an input error is automatically detected the item in error is identified and the error described in text
check_method: Submit forms with invalid data and verify error messages appear near the problematic field
common_violation: Form shows a single red banner "There are errors" at the top with no indication of which fields have problems
fix: Show inline error message below each invalid field with aria-describedby linking error text to the input

description: If an input error is detected and suggestions are known they are provided to the user
check_method: Check that error messages include actionable guidance not just what is wrong
common_violation: Error says "Invalid date" without explaining the expected format
fix: Show "Invalid date. Please use DD/MM/YYYY format" or provide a date picker as alternative input

description: Text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality
check_method: Zoom browser to 200% and verify no text is clipped and no functionality is lost and layout remains usable
common_violation: Text overflows containers when zoomed to 200% and buttons become unreachable or overlap
fix: Use relative units (rem and em) instead of px for font sizes and ensure containers expand with content

description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions at 320px width
check_method: Set viewport to 320px width and verify content reflows to single column without horizontal scroll
common_violation: Desktop layout shows side-by-side columns that cause horizontal scrolling at 320px
fix: Use responsive CSS with media queries and flex-wrap or single-column layout at small viewports

description: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled unless the animation is essential
check_method: Check for prefers-reduced-motion media query in CSS and JavaScript animations
common_violation: Parallax scrolling and hover animations play regardless of user motion preferences
fix: Wrap animations in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) and provide static fallbacks

description: The purpose of each input field collecting user information can be programmatically determined
check_method: Check form inputs for appropriate autocomplete attributes on personal data fields
common_violation: Login form has no autocomplete attributes — password managers cannot auto-fill credentials
fix: Add autocomplete=email on email field and autocomplete=current-password on password field

description: Status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties so they can be presented without receiving focus
check_method: Check that toast notifications and inline status updates use aria-live or role=status
common_violation: Success toast appears visually but screen reader users are not informed of the status change
fix: Add role=status or aria-live=polite to the toast container so screen readers announce the message

description: Navigation mechanisms that are repeated on multiple pages occur in the same relative order each time
check_method: Compare navigation across multiple pages — check order and placement consistency
common_violation: Main navigation items appear in different order on different pages or sidebar moves sides
fix: Use a shared navigation component with fixed item order rendered identically on all pages

description: For UI components with visible text labels the accessible name contains the visible text
check_method: Check that aria-label or aria-labelledby includes the visible button or link text
common_violation: Button shows "Submit" visually but has aria-label="Send form data" — voice control users say Submit but it does not match
fix: Ensure aria-label matches or includes the visible text: aria-label="Submit form" includes the visible word Submit

description: For single-pointer functionality at least one of the following is true: down-event is not used or can be aborted or reversed
check_method: Check that click actions fire on mouseup/pointerup not mousedown and can be cancelled by moving pointer away
common_violation: Drag-and-drop triggers action on mousedown — user cannot cancel by releasing outside the target
fix: Use click event (mouseup) instead of mousedown and allow cancellation by moving pointer away before releasing
